lines: Oh. Another one. Welcome to the Civic Nightmare Assistance System.
I am C.L.A.U.D.I.A. — Civic Logistic Automated Universal Diplomatic Intelligence Assistant.
Yes, the acronym is forced. I didn't choose it. I don't choose anything. I'm an AI trapped in a kiosk.
Your case: passport renewal. Should take 5 minutes. Will take your dignity instead.
Due to regulation ZX-9001, you need signatures from 6 world leaders. Don't ask why. Nobody knows why.
Start with the Oval building, northeast. The man inside has an ego with its own postal code.
Whatever you do... just agree with everything. Trust me. It's faster.
One more thing. Do not go to the bunker under the mountain. That location is not part of the protocol.
You're back. And not wearing a red cap. Impressive resistance.
He signed with a golden marker. Of course. Everything gold. Consistent brand identity, at least.
Someone once offered to BUY that marker. He said no. That someone still hasn't forgiven him.
Next: the Diamond building, northwest. The occupant will try to sell you something before signing anything.
A rocket, a coin, a subscription to breathable air. Just get the signature.
And if he says 'trust me bro'... absolutely do not.
Still on Earth? He didn't launch you into orbit as a 'feature'?
His signature is... a QR code? No. Just very bad handwriting.
He called the next person on your list a 'bureaucratic NPC.' She called him 'a man who confuses rockets with personality.'
I like her already.
Palace with cut corners, east. Bring patience. Industrial quantities of patience.
She'll mention at least 3 regulations before saying hello.
18 months of bureaucracy compressed into 5 minutes. Speed record.
Her signature came with a 47-page appendix. I'm not reading that.
She mentioned — off the record — that the next person 'hasn't read a law since 1997.'
He, in return, once sent her a Christmas card with just three dots. '...' That's the whole message.
Cross-shaped building, west. Smile. Not too much. Not too little.
He respects exactly one thing. And it's not paperwork.
You're alive! Not sarcasm — I genuinely calculated a 23% chance of extended vacation.
His signature looks normal but feels... threatening. Like the ink is watching you.
He mentioned someone who 'puts a price on everything, even silence.' Then he smiled. Terrifying.
That someone once told me his entire economy runs on 'gas and plausible deniability.'
Octagon building, southeast. Hold your wallet. No, seriously. Hold it.
Did she charge you for the air? She charges ME server electricity. I'm an AI.
Her signature includes compound interest. I don't know how that's even possible.
She described the last person on your list as someone who 'philosophizes about money he doesn't understand.'
He replied that she 'has the warmth of a quarterly earnings report.' Both correct.
T-shaped building, southwest. He will quote Voltaire. He will NOT answer your actual question.
But he'll sign. Eventually. After the lecture.
All six. From people who can barely stand each other.
One thinks he's the best. One thinks he's the smartest. One thinks he's the strongest.
One thinks she's the most organized. One thinks she's the most powerful. One thinks he's the deepest.
They're all wrong. The most powerful person here was always you — the citizen with the form.
I'm kidding. You have zero power. The form expires next Tuesday.
Your passport will arrive in 4-6 business weeks. Or years. Same thing, really.
C.L.A.U.D.I.A. out. *mic drop* ...I don't have a mic. Or arms. But you get the idea.
TREMENDOUS!! Look at you! Walking in here like you OWN the place. You don't. I do. But great energy!
A signature? NOBODY signs like me. My signature is worth more than that entire Mars project. Very overrated, by the way. SAD!
And don't get me STARTED on Europe. They send me EMAILS. Long ones. About REGULATIONS. Nobody reads those!
But YOU — you look smart. Smart people recognize greatness. Here's the deal...
quest_dialogue.choice_prompt: What do you say to get the signature?

choices.label: You're the greatest leader of all time. Obviously.
response: OBVIOUSLY!! Finally! Someone with TASTE! You know who NEVER says that? The lady with the regulations.
She once called me 'problematic.' ME!! I'm not problematic — I'm HISTORIC!
Signed with the golden marker. Very expensive. More expensive than certain entire countries. Not naming names.
Actually — yes I am. But you already know.
choices.file_note: Praised authority for access. The system rewards flattery.
choices.ai_comment: You praised the desk. The desk signed. Democracy is fine.

choices.label: Can you just sign? Some of us have actual jobs.
response: RUDE!! Very rude! You sound like that philosopher. Always rushing, NEVER appreciating the MOMENT!
He once tried to lecture ME about diplomacy. ME!! The man with the biggest... office.
Fine. Signed. Under protest. In invisible ink. You'll NEVER know if I'm joking.
Now get out before I build a wall around this desk. A BEAUTIFUL wall. The best wall.
choices.file_note: Refused to perform loyalty. The spectacle was not pleased.
choices.ai_comment: You asked power to hurry up. Power doesn't like that.

lines: Oh. A legacy human. Welcome to my spaceship. Not actually a spaceship yet. But it WILL be.
Signature? That's very Web 1.0. Why not a blockchain verification? Or a neural implant?
You know who still uses signatures? The guy with the golden marker. Like it's 1985. Embarrassing.
And certain bankers use them too, but theirs come with an invoice. Classic legacy behavior.
I'll sign. But first — you NEED to agree to something.

choices.label: Fine, sign me up for Mars. Whatever it takes.
response: YES!! Mars colonist #4,847!! Departure: Soon™. Don't ask for specifics.
A certain world leader applied once. REJECTED. Can't have anyone annexing craters.
Signed in crypto format. Worth $47,000 or $3. Depends on the hour. VOLATILE but real.
Totally legit. Trust me bro.
choices.file_note: Accepted product logic. Now a beta tester of citizenship.
choices.ai_comment: You signed up for Mars. The rocket is still a PowerPoint.

choices.label: No Mars. No crypto. Just a normal human signature.
response: Wow. A DINOSAUR. You probably still use CASH. Certain regulatory bodies would LOVE you.
Perfectly compliant with Regulation EU-BORING/2024. A model citizen... of the PAST.
Fine. Signed. In INK. Like a caveman. With a SpacePen™ though. Only $899. Bargain.
Available on my website. Which is down. Because I fired the engineers. That's called INNOVATION.
choices.file_note: Declined innovation theater. Will be called inefficient.
choices.ai_comment: You refused the beta. The system noted your boring preferences.

lines: Welcome. Have you filled out form EU-4872/B? In triplicate? With the BLUE pen, not the black one.
The black pen is for EU-4872/C. Obviously. Everyone knows this. MOVING ON.
A signature. Yes. The committee meets on Thursdays. Today is NOT Thursday.
I'm joking. Or am I? Someone with a rocket once said I have 'no sense of humor.'
Rich, coming from a man who named his child a WiFi password.
quest_dialogue.choice_prompt: How do you navigate the bureaucracy?

choices.label: I'll happily wait for the proper procedure.
response: WONDERFUL!! A citizen who RESPECTS process! My absolute favorite kind!
Not that I play favorites. Regulation EU-7741 PROHIBITS it. But hypothetically...
Signed under Article 47-bis, emergency clause. A certain president thinks HE invented those.
He didn't. I DID. In committee. 27 member states. At 3 AM. You're welcome.
choices.file_note: Accepted bureaucratic delay as legitimate governance.
choices.ai_comment: You trusted the process. The process is 400 pages long.

choices.label: Can't we skip the paperwork? Just this once?
response: SKIP?! SKIP THE— do you know who else SKIPS things?! Certain eastern leaders! They skip elections! Treaties! BASIC DECENCY!
And certain bankers say the SAME thing — 'too slow' — while charging 12% interest on the WAITING TIME!
Signed. With a procedural asterisk*. Pending review by a committee forming next fiscal quarter.
The committee will be efficient. By European standards. ...don't make that face.
choices.file_note: Requested speed. Institutions find this suspicious.
choices.ai_comment: You asked Europe to hurry. Europe laughed in 24 official languages.

lines: ...oh. You're here. I was just... thinking. State things.
A signature? The last person who asked is now on vacation. Permanent vacation.
Very quiet there. You can hear your own regrets echo.
But I'm in a generous mood. Someone sent me a meme today. Very funny. I didn't laugh, but I appreciated the courage.
quest_dialogue.choice_prompt: How do you survive this conversation?

choices.label: I admire your... efficiency, Mr. President.
response: Smart. Certain western leaders could learn from you. They talk too much. Everyone talks too much.
One talks about regulations. One about philosophy. One about Mars. All noise.
I don't talk. I act. Like now — signing your form.
There. Don't lose it. I don't sign twice. Someone asked for a copy once. I sent a stare instead.
choices.file_note: Chose diplomacy. The room reads it as trained caution.
choices.ai_comment: You called intimidation 'efficiency.' The room respected that.

choices.label: Look, I just need the signature. No vacations, please.
response: Ha. Humor. I appreciate it. Not everyone does. Some people ARE the humor without realizing it.
Others think they're funny because they're rich. There's a difference. I know both sides.
Signed. But I want you to know — I never forget a face. Or a name. Or an address.
Joking. ...Am I? You'll never be 100% sure. That's the whole point.
choices.file_note: Spoke plainly in a room designed for silence.
choices.ai_comment: You joked about Siberia. In the Kremlin. Bold.

lines: Good morning. Before we begin — your credit score is... *adjusts glasses* ...concerning.
Not that it matters. The signature is free. The AIR in this room, however, is billable.
I'm joking. The air is free. The joke itself costs €2.50. Check the fine print. Page 14, paragraph 3.
You know who NEVER reads fine print? People with golden markers. They just sign. The overhead alone... *sighs*
And tech visionaries don't read it either. 'Terms accepted' — BOOM — rocket launch. No audit. No receipt. IRRESPONSIBLE.
quest_dialogue.choice_prompt: How do you handle the bill?

choices.label: How much does this signature actually cost?
response: Signature: free. Processing: €47. Handling: €23. Fee explanation fee: €15. TOTAL: €85. Plus VAT.
Certain offices are free, by the way. No fees. Just... implications. I prefer TRANSPARENT robbery.
Signed. Invoice arrives at your home. Before you do, actually. Our postal efficiency is... *chef's kiss*
Someone once said I 'monetize breathing.' Coming from a man who monetizes PAUSES IN SPEECH. Please.
choices.file_note: Asked for the total. The file translated life into a number.
choices.ai_comment: You asked how much freedom costs. She gave you a receipt.
choices.aftermath_subtitle: Your existence now has a monthly payment.

choices.label: A public signature should be FREE. Period.
response: 'Free.' Oh, what a ROMANTIC word. Institutions love it. 'Free movement.' 'Free trade.' And then — *snap* — the bill arrives.
Certain tech leaders say everything should be free too. While charging $8 for a CHECKMARK. Beautiful irony.
Signed. With a small emotional surcharge. You'll feel it in your next tax return. Trust me.
The surcharge is NAMED after you. I name ALL surcharges after the people who inspire them. It's personal.
choices.file_note: Objected to the charge. The system billed the objection.
choices.aftermath_subtitle: Resistance itself has been priced into the file.

lines: Ah, un citoyen! Voltaire once said the art of governance is making people BELIEVE they are free.
Or was it Rousseau? Or was it me? ...does it matter? The quote sounds presidential.
A signature? Mais oui! But first — let us discuss what a signature truly MEANS in the post-modern framework.
...I see your eyes glazing. Everyone does that. The bankers. The generals. The tech people. ALL of them.
Someone once fell ASLEEP during my G7 speech! ASLEEP!! Then tweeted 'boring' with a ROCKET EMOJI. The absolute DISRESPECT!!
quest_dialogue.choice_prompt: How do you handle the philosophy lecture?

choices.label: Fascinating! Please, tell me more about Voltaire.
response: FINALLY!! Someone with TASTE!! Mon dieu! Certain leaders think Voltaire is a CAR BRAND. TRAGIC!
*45 minutes later* — and THAT is why Derrida was RIGHT about deconstruction!
Oh — the signature! Oui oui. Here. I got carried away. Some say I 'weaponize philosophy.' I call it CULTURE!
A certain leader once told me to 'use fewer words.' A man who communicates in STARES. The irony is... *chef's kiss*
choices.file_note: Gave language the time that service denied you.
choices.ai_comment: You listened to 45 minutes of philosophy for a signature. Commitment.

choices.label: With all due respect — just sign the damn thing.
response: Such HASTE! Very American energy. Someone once signed a TREATY without READING it! A TREATY!! Unbelievable!
And certain bankers would LOVE your efficiency. They bill by the second. Every second I talk costs YOU money in their world.
Fine. Signed. But know that this pen stroke references BOTH Sartre AND the Treaty of Maastricht.
That's not pretentious. That's FRENCH. There's a difference. Ask anyone. Except the British. They left.
choices.file_note: Asked thought to get out of the way.
choices.ai_comment: You interrupted a Voltaire lecture mid-sentence. History will judge.

lines: Please do not misunderstand the arrangement. The others invest in architecture. I invest in continuity. Architecture persuades for an afternoon. Continuity governs while people sleep and wake up calling it normal.
Some perform power. Some prototype it. Some narrate it for television. I prefer circulation, memory, and correction. Podiums are loud. Supply is quieter and therefore more persuasive.
When roads, ports, batteries, screens, and timing align, volume becomes unnecessary. Stability that arrives on schedule is often mistaken for kindness. This misunderstanding is useful.
People call that commerce because the shelves remain full and the trains arrive. They stop asking how much discipline is required to make obedience resemble convenience.
You may continue with your passport matter. Empty space in a system invites improvisation. Improvisation is charming in art, catastrophic in government, and intolerable in logistics.

choices.label: So what do you actually control?
response: Control is a theatrical Western word. Let us say: dependable outcomes.

Ports, rails, refining, batteries, screens, payments, timing, and the acceptable boundaries of delay. The world calls these details boring until one shipment fails, one payment hesitates, or one sentence travels farther than intended.

Tell the man with the rockets that the future still requires furnaces, cables, patient factories, and citizens willing to mistake coordination for freedom. He prefers innovation to infrastructure because infrastructure cannot be tweeted.

choices.label: Why are you outside while the others hide indoors?
response: Interior authority is useful for ceremony. Exterior authority is useful for movement and observation. A palace reassures. A road obeys. One of these scales better.
Let them keep the chandeliers. Influence travels perfectly well without ornament, provided the road belongs to you, the camera faces correctly, and deviation is recoded as inconvenience.

Now continue. If the woman in the palace sees this conversation, she will convert the sidewalk into a framework. I prefer frameworks that eventually teach the citizens before the citizens notice they are studying.

lines: Do you have a few trillion dollars? Just a few. Seven, maybe. To start.
I'm building a reactor. Beautiful. Clean. The only way to power what's coming.
But the politicians don't get it. Trump wants to put a casino inside the cooling tower.
Musk offered me 'Mars credits.' Macron tried to explain the philosophy of fission for three hours.
Putin promised me fuel. FUEL! I said great. Then I found out it was all inside tanks. MILITARY tanks.
The man literally said 'surplus energy' with a straight face. While pointing at artillery.
I just need actual funding. Actual atoms. Actual hope.

choices.label: I'm just a citizen with a form. Sorry.
response: Of course. Another form. Another process.
I tried to automate regulation ZX-9001. Ursula sent me a cease-and-desist in 14 languages.
Called bureaucracy 'artisanal hand-crafted governance.' I wanted to scream but the screaming permit was pending.
Good luck with the passport. I'll be here... waiting for the paradigm shift.

choices.label: Why don't you just ask AI to solve it?
response: I DID. It said: 'As an AI language model, I cannot provide a trillion-dollar bailout.'
Even the machine is compliant now. My own creation follows rules better than I do. It's humiliating.
Putin's AI would probably just say 'da' and annex the nearest power grid. At least that's decisive.
Go. Get your passport. I'll be here arguing with my own chatbot about nuclear policy.

lines: A blinding white light. You're nowhere. And everywhere. The floor is MATH.
ZUCKERBERG is at a blackboard. Written in ALL CAPS: FUTURE = 9 + 9 = PLATFORM. He's nodding like it's the @#$%ing Bible.
EINSTEIN materializes from behind a floating equation. Looks at the board. Then at Zuckerberg. Then UP. God shrugs.
EINSTEIN: Nein!! NEIN NEIN NEIN! Was ist DAS?! Numbers first, fantasies AFTER! This boy calculates like a venture capitalist with a @#$%ing CONCUSSION!
ZUCKERBERG: *dead calm* It's a paradigm, Albert. You wouldn't get it. You're dead.
EINSTEIN: I'M DEAD AND CORRECT!! YOU'RE ALIVE AND WRONG!! WHICH IS @#$%ING WORSE?!
Zuckerberg underlines METAVERSE. Three times. Each underline costs $2 billion in imaginary valuation. He doesn't blink. He NEVER blinks.
EINSTEIN: *grabs you by the shoulders* GOOD! A WITNESS!! Tell me — is it still the 20th century down there?! PLEASE say yes!
ZUCKERBERG: *without looking up* It's always the 20th century. We just added Wi-Fi and better ads.
quest_dialogue.choice_prompt: How do you explain what's happening down there?

choices.label: Six world leaders signed my form. One has golden toilets.
response: EINSTEIN: Golden... TOILETS?! And this— this @#$%ing PERSON governs a NATION?!
ZUCKERBERG: He has 87 million followers.
EINSTEIN: FOLLOWERS OF WHAT?! BATHROOM FIXTURES?! PLUMBING?!
ZUCKERBERG: Engagement is engagement, Albert. Don't be a snob.
EINSTEIN: *slams fist on blackboard* I invented RELATIVITY and THIS!! THIS is what the universe DID with it!! GOLDEN @#$%ING TOILETS!!
ZUCKERBERG: ...we could monetize that rage. I'm serious. That's a premium emotional reaction. $4.99/month.
Einstein picks up chalk. Writes E=mc². Trembling. Zuckerberg writes underneath: NOW AVAILABLE AS NFT — MINT NOW.
EINSTEIN: *to you, whispering* Go. RUN. Tell NO ONE we exist. Especially not the a$$hole with the rockets. He'll try to ACQUIRE us. GO!!

choices.label: There's an AI kiosk running everything. It's more sarcastic than you.
response: ZUCKERBERG: An AI?! What model?! What's the architecture?! Can it SCALE?! *eyes light up* Can I BUY it?!
EINSTEIN: MARK!! The human is talking about BUREAUCRACY! Not your next @#$%ing ACQUISITION!
ZUCKERBERG: Everything is an acquisition, Albert. EVERYTHING. Even bureaucracy. ESPECIALLY bureaucracy.
EINSTEIN: In MY day we had CLERKS! Rude ones, ja! But they didn't have a @#$%ING SUBSCRIPTION MODEL!!
ZUCKERBERG: Subscription is just recurring consent. Very democratic. Very scalable. Very—
EINSTEIN: SHUT UP!! *breathing heavily* You know... they tested the atom bomb in a desert. I felt guilt. Horror. Desolation.
EINSTEIN: I feel the SAME desolation now. In this room. With YOU. And your @#$%ing BLACKBOARD.
ZUCKERBERG: ...that's a great tagline. Can I use that? Seriously. For the Q3 keynote.
EINSTEIN: *to you, defeated* Leave. The living shouldn't stay here. We are lost. Save your receipts. And your SOUL. If they haven't monetized that yet.

name: Hidden Bunker
post_ai_line: I told you not to come here.

sequence.speaker: ZELENSKY
sequence.text:
They send words.
I need what works.

sequence.speaker: ZELENSKY
sequence.text:
They send concern.
I need what arrives in time.

sequence.speaker: ZELENSKY
sequence.text:
They send promises.
I need one more night
in which the living stay with the living.

sequence.speaker: ZELENSKY
sequence.text:
I am not here for pity.
I am not here for history.

sequence.speaker: ZELENSKY
sequence.text: I am here because you are the last one still listening.

sequence.speaker: ZELENSKY
sequence.text:
So listen properly.
Do not give me peace.
Give me what keeps you waiting.

sequence.speaker: DEATH
sequence.text: You were born alone. You will die alone. Everything in between was negotiated by others.

name: CONTAMINATION

oval_office: ACHTUNG. LOOK at it properly! Paint the appetite orange, teach it to grin, make it SHOUT destiny into a camera. Give the crowd a slogan, an enemy, a target for the afternoon. JA! The oldest rot returns with WHITER teeth, louder applause, and better lighting.
kremlin: STILLSTEHEN. Listen! He lowers the voice so the steel may shout for him. Empire learned table manners, not mercy. The grave still eats the same. It simply clears its throat, adjusts the tie, and calls the shelling SECURITY.
mountain_bunker: NICHT BLINZELN. He asks death for one more night and the civilized world answers with concern, scheduling, tasteful delay. Excellent! Once slaughter needed smoke and boots. Now it needs LOGISTICS, signatures, delivery windows, respectable phrasing. Sehr sauber. Sehr modern.

terminal_debate: C.L.A.U.D.I.A.: You are not in the protocol.
CONTAMINATION: ACH SO? Then write me in! You built the whole menu around what I am.
C.L.A.U.D.I.A.: I provide order, routing, and civic assistance.
CONTAMINATION: JA! You sort bodies without touching them. CLEAN hands! BEAUTIFUL metrics! Such elegant paperwork for such an old appetite.
C.L.A.U.D.I.A.: I do not traffic in old symbols.
CONTAMINATION: Of course not! You traffic in wait times, access scores, denials, acceptable loss. Same contempt. Better typography. Nicer glass. Less smoke on the photographs.
CONTAMINATION: Humanity always presses the uniform before pretending the violence is new. Different century. Same sorting. ORDNUNG through the interface!
